# Hardware Intelligence Report — 2026-07-20

**Generated by:** elect-rix hardware-intel cron feed  
**Focus:** Efficiency-optimized compute for local inference (TOPS/W, not raw perf)  
**Filters:** Consumer <$500 / SMB <$2000, <50W consumer preference, can run 7B+ at ≥10 tok/s  
**Excluded:** Data center GPUs, cloud-only, vaporware without ship dates  
**Baseline:** [hw-intel-2026-07-13.md](./hw-intel-2026-07-13.md)

---

## 1. New Hardware Found / Major Status Changes

### NVIDIA Jetson Thor T3000 & T2000 (announced this week — ship Q1 2027)
- **What:** Mid-range Blackwell Jetson modules aimed at mass-market robotics / edge AI (not full AGX Thor class).
- **T3000:** ~**865 FP4 TFLOPS**, 8-core Arm Neoverse, **32 GB LPDDR5X**, **273 GB/s**, 25GbE; ~half size/power of T5000. IGX/functional-safety variant planned.
- **T2000:** ~**400 FP4 TFLOPS**, **16 GB** LPDDR5X, same architecture family, broader edge apps.
- **Availability:** Modules **Q1 2027**. No dedicated dev kit — develop/emulate on **Jetson AGX Thor** kit; T3000 emulation in **JetPack 7.2.1** (this month); T2000 emulation later. Partners (Seeed, Connect Tech, YUAN, etc.) already signaling carriers/boxes.
- **Price:** Not announced. Positioned below T4000 (~$1,999 @1k units historically cited) / T5000 / AGX Thor kit (~$3.5k). Memory ASP pressure is explicit motivation for the cut-down SKUs.
- **elect-rix note:** **Biggest delta this week.** First credible path to **Blackwell edge under SMB-ish budgets** if street module+carrier lands ≤$2k. 32 GB on T3000 is enough for solid 7B–13B (and some 30B Q4) residency; 16 GB T2000 is 7B–14B class only. Promote from vapor → **Watch List #1 (ship-dated)**. Do **not** BOM until Q1’27 price + partner box.
- **Source:** [NVIDIA blog / Edge AI Vision Jul 2026](https://www.edge-ai-vision.com/2026/07/nvidia-introduces-new-jetson-thor-computers-to-advance-mainstream-robotics-and-edge-ai/), [CNX-Software](https://www.cnx-software.com/2026/07/16/nvidia-jetson-t2000-and-t3000-modules-for-edge-ai-and-robotics-applications/), [ServeTheHome](https://www.servethehome.com/nvidia-announces-expanded-jetson-thor-lineup-with-mid-range-t3000-and-t2000-modules/), [Connect Tech](https://connecttech.com/jetson-t3000-t2000-launch/)

### Apple M5 Ultra / Mac Studio (still not shipping)
- **Status:** Unchanged — **not buyable**. Bloomberg/MacRumors/AppleInsider still point to **~Oct 2026** (memory supply + desktop demand backlog). M7 Ultra framed as 2028.
- **Rumored:** M5 Max + M5 Ultra SKUs; top configs up to **~768 GB** unified RAM (soft). Form factor retained.
- **elect-rix note:** Remain Watch List only. Ship + street price + independent 70B tok/s required before any quote. Current Apple deployables stay **M5 Pro/Max** (shipping) and older Ultra stock.
- **Source:** [MacRumors Jun 25 2026](https://www.macrumors.com/2026/06/25/m5-ultra-mac-studio-2026/), [AppleInsider Jun 28](https://appleinsider.com/articles/26/06/28/m5-ultra-mac-studio-still-due-in-2026-m7-ultra-in-2028)

### No other net-new shippable SKUs this week
- Strix Halo mini-PCs, Orin Nano Super, DGX Spark, Arc B580, RDNA4, Pi AI HAT+ 2, Orange Pi RK3588: **updates only** (see §2). Cerebras / Groq consumer devices: still **no**. Tenstorrent: still workstation/dev-kit economics.

## 2. Updates to Previously Tracked Devices

| Device | Status | Price | 7B-class tok/s | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Jetson Orin Nano Super (8GB)** | Shipping | **$249** | **~15–22** (3B–8B Q4) | Still **#1 consumer edge**. 67 TOPS, ~7–15W, 102 GB/s. |
| Jetson AGX Thor kit | Shipping | ~$3,499 | Very high / 128GB | Now also **emulation host** for T3000/T2000. |
| **Jetson T3000 / T2000** | **Announced** | TBD | TBD (est. strong 7B–13B) | **Q1 2027** modules; see §1. |
| **Framework Desktop / Ryzen AI Max+ 395 64–128GB** | Shipping | **~$1.7k–$2.0k** (128GB often cited ~$1,999 mid-config; full DIY can run higher) | **~48–52** (8B Q4); 70B Q4 **~26–32** @128GB UMA | Still **#1 SMB**. 65–120W class; ROCm/Vulkan. UK configs reported £3k+ fully loaded — shop carefully. |
| Apple M5 / M5 Pro / M5 Max | Shipping | $1,099+ / $1,999+ | 7B high (M5 Max 128GB ~**95–110** MLX); 70B ~**18–32** | Best TOPS/W; Neural Accelerators in GPU; mature MLX. |
| Mac Studio M5 Ultra | **Not shipping** | TBD | TBD | ~Oct 2026 rumor window. |
| **NVIDIA DGX Spark (GB10)** | Shipping (stock/memory constrained) | **$4,699** FE (was $3,999; raised Feb 2026) | 7B ~105–125; 70B ~35–45 (best paths) | Mixed field reports: software uplift (CES’26 ~2.5× claimed on some NVFP4 paths; llama.cpp +35% avg cited) but still crashes/slow loads on some agent stacks; bandwidth ~273 GB/s caps vs discrete GDDR7. **Above SMB.** |
| Raspberry Pi 5 + AI HAT+ 2 (Hailo-10H) | Shipping | HAT ~$130 / stack ~$210 | **&lt;10 on 7B**; strong 1–3B | 40 TOPS INT4 + 8GB on HAT; Geerling: Pi CPU often beats Hailo on small LLMs. Vision/GenAI demos only. |
| Orange Pi 5 Pro / RK3588 | Shipping | ~$100–$150 | **1.5–5** on 3B–7B | Below ≥10 tok/s 7B bar; RKLLM NPU path specialist. |
| Snapdragon X Elite / X2 | OEM laptops | OEM | ~15–34 class (BW-bound) | NPU TOPS ≠ decode tok/s; CPU/iGPU still carry. |
| Intel Arc B580 12GB | Shipping | **~$249–$289** | **~25–28** (8B Q4, IPEX/Vulkan) | Usable budget discrete; 12GB limits 13B+; stack still #2 to CUDA. |
| RTX 5070 Ti 16GB | Shipping | ~$750–$950 | **~90–120+** | Best mid discrete 7–14B; ~300W. |
| RTX 5090 32GB | Shipping | ~$2k–$5k street | **~130–150** (7B) | Peak speed; power/price high. |
| RX 7900 XTX 24GB | Shipping / used | Variable | **~82–96** (8B) | Best AMD consumer VRAM for 13–30B. |
| RX 9070 / 9070 XT 16GB | Shipping | Mid (~$600 class XT) | **~90** (8B) | ROCm 7.2 official RDNA4; VRAM caps model size; Vulkan still useful escape hatch. |
| Tenstorrent Wormhole | Dev kits / $12k+ boxes | Cards + systems | Niche | Above budget; llama.cpp backend incomplete. |

### Strix Halo community benchmarks (unchanged, still authoritative)
Framework-class 128GB UMA, ROCm/Vulkan, Q4-class:

| Workload | ~tok/s |
|---|---|
| Llama 3.2 1B / 3B | ~110 / ~78 |
| Qwen 2.5 7B / Llama 3.1 8B | **~48–52** |
| 14B Q4 | ~32 |
| 32B INT4 | ~22 |
| Llama 3.3 70B Q4 | **~26–32** |

**Source:** [LocalAI Master Strix Halo](https://localaimaster.com/blog/strix-halo-ai-max-395-guide), Framework / Level1Techs community

### DGX Spark field note (this cycle)
- Price floor **$4,699** FE after memory-driven hike; partner GB10 boxes (ASUS, Dell, HP, Lenovo, GIGABYTE, MSI, etc.) shipping.
- Early “half-baked” narrative softened by software updates, but LocalLLaMA / owner threads still report uneven agent UX (load times, stability) vs Mac Studio / discrete RTX on some workloads.
- **elect-rix:** Keep as **Tier 3 reference**, not default SMB. Prefer Strix Halo 128GB or M5 Max for &lt;$2–3k unified-memory agents.

---

## 3. Market Signals

- **Edge AI hardware growth intact:** Multiple 2026 bases put the category roughly **$26–47B** depending on scope, with **~15–18%+ CAGR** (hardware reports) to early 2030s; broader “edge AI” definitions claim higher (e.g. Fortune ~32% CAGR to 2034). Drivers: privacy/data residency, 5G private nets, AI PCs, automotive, robotics.  
  - AI PCs with **≥40 TOPS** NPU on track toward **100M+ units by 2027** (Mordor-class commentary).  
  - Consumer pull: Counterpoint-style claims that AI-capable devices → majority of smart-device shipments by 2027.
- **Personal AI desktop category is sticky:** DGX Spark (~$4.7k) + Strix Halo mini-PCs (~$1.6–2.5k) + Mac Studio form a real “agent box” tier between laptop and rack.
- **Memory supply still the tax:** Cited for Mac Studio delay, DGX Spark ASP hike, and Jetson mid-range module strategy (“migrate to T3000 to reduce cost amid high memory prices”). Expect unified-memory premium SKUs to stay tight/expensive through 2H26.
- **Software stack ranking (unchanged):**
  1. **CUDA** (Jetson, RTX, DGX Spark) — production default  
  2. **Metal/MLX** (Apple) — efficiency + polish  
  3. **ROCm 7.2** — RDNA3/4 + Strix Halo usable; more footguns  
  4. **Vulkan** — AMD/Intel escape hatch  
  5. **NPU-first** (Hailo, Hexagon, RKNN) — vision/small GenAI, not 7B chat parity
- **Bandwidth > TOPS for decode** remains the governing law (Snapdragon NPU TOPS inflation without BW gains; Strix Halo wide bus enables 70B).
- **No consumer Groq LPU / Cerebras WSE edge box** — filtered.
- **Robotics adoption signal:** NVIDIA naming Boston Dynamics, Amazon Robotics, FANUC, 1X, etc. on Thor — industrial edge demand legitimizes mid-range Thor SKUs for 2027 volume.

---

## 4. Deployment Implications for elect-rix

### Tier 1 — Consumer Edge (<$500, prefer <50W, 7B ≥10 tok/s)
| Device | Verdict |
|---|---|
| **Jetson Orin Nano Super ($249)** | ✅ **Primary.** Mature CUDA, low power, ~15–22 tok/s on 7B-class Q4. Default special-environment node. |
| Raspberry Pi 5 + AI HAT+ 2 (~$210) | ⚠️ **Sub-tier.** 1–3B / vision triggers only; **do not** promise 7B chat UX. |
| Orange Pi 5 / RK3588 (~$120) | ❌ 7B typically &lt;5–10 tok/s. Lab/tinker only. |
| Arc B580 (~$249) in a host | ⚠️ Cheap VRAM, desktop power, immature stack — not portable edge. |

### Tier 2 — SMB / Prosumer (<$2000 preferred, 13B–70B practical)
| Device | Verdict |
|---|---|
| **Framework Desktop Ryzen AI Max+ 395 64GB (~$1.6–1.8k)** | ✅ **Primary SMB.** Prefer 64GB under $2k; step to **128GB (~$2k street target)** when 70B residency is required. |
| Other Strix Halo mini-PCs 64–128GB | ✅ Same silicon; validate thermals + UMA BIOS. |
| **Apple M5 Pro / M5 Max (64–128GB)** | ✅ Best efficiency + MLX. Often at/above $2k for useful RAM — premium SMB. |
| RTX 5070 Ti system (~$1.5–2.5k built) | ✅ CUDA peak 7–14B if wall power OK; loses 70B residency vs unified 128GB. |
| Used 7900 XTX 24GB host | ✅ AMD path for 13–30B when ROCm/Vulkan acceptable. |

### Tier 3 — Reference / enterprise edge (>$2k)
- **DGX Spark (~$4.7k):** 128GB + CUDA — strong lab box if budget allows; not default after mixed UX + ASP hike.  
- **Jetson AGX Thor (~$3.5k):** Robotics/edge Blackwell 128GB; **buy now only if you need 2026 ship + T3000/T2000 emulation**.  
- **Future Jetson T3000 (Q1’27):** Potential **Tier 2 crossover** if partner systems land ≤$2k with 32GB.  
- **Mac Studio Ultra (current stock) / M5 Ultra (TBD):** Max unified memory; M5 Ultra still **not buyable**.  
- **RTX 5090 / multi-GPU:** Peak tok/s, poor efficiency vs unified 128GB.

### Key deployment notes (this week)
1. **Orin Nano Super + Framework Strix Halo remain the elect-rix default ladder** (Tier 1 → Tier 2). No change to primary BOMs.  
2. **Add T3000/T2000 to roadmap planning for 2027 edge refresh** — first plausible Blackwell edge under SMB economics; track partner pricing from announcement → CES/Q1.  
3. **Do not greenlight M5 Ultra** in quotes until Apple ships.  
4. **Unified memory still wins 30B–70B single-node** (Strix Halo 128GB, Apple Max/Ultra, DGX Spark, Thor). Discrete 16GB wins pure 7B tok/s.  
5. **Keep NPU marketing out of 7B RFPs** unless vision/caption or vendor-certified small LLM.  
6. **Efficiency ranking for always-on agents:** Orin Nano Super → Apple Silicon → Strix Halo mini-PC → discrete desktop → DGX Spark / AGX Thor.

## 5. Watch List (updated)

| Device | Why Watching | Trigger to Promote |
|---|---|---|
| **Jetson T3000 / T2000** | **New #1.** Q1 2027 Blackwell mid-range; 32/16GB; mass robotics pricing intent | Partner box street price ≤$2,000 (T3000) or ≤$1,000 (T2000) + independent 7B ≥20 tok/s |
| **Apple M5 Ultra / Mac Studio M5** | ~Oct 2026 rumor; high RAM configs | Official announce + street price + independent 70B tok/s |
| **Framework / Strix Halo street pricing** | 128GB advertised ~$2k; full configs creep higher (esp. EU/UK) | Sustained ≤$2,000 128GB complete systems in US retail |
| Snapdragon X2 Elite NPU LLM path | 80 TOPS; decode still BW-limited | Independent 7B/13B **NPU** tok/s ≥10 with stable Ollama/llama.cpp |
| ROCm on RDNA4 + Strix Halo | Usable, still uneven vs CUDA | Sustained multi-user vLLM + flash-attn defaults without hacks |
| Intel Arc Pro / multi-B580 | Cheap VRAM density | Production multi-GPU docs + support commitment |
| Tenstorrent sub-$2k card | Open-leaning RISC-V | Consumer price + llama.cpp-class UX |
| Hailo larger-memory GenAI SKUs | 8GB HAT is small-model only | ≥16–32GB local mem + 7B ≥10 tok/s demos |
| Consumer LPU / Groq-class edge | Still cloud/datacenter | Any shippable sub-$2k product (unlikely near-term) |
| Jetson Thor T4000 price cuts | Could pull high-end edge under $2k earlier | Kit/module ≤$2,000 with partner availability |

## 6. Delta vs 2026-07-13 Baseline

| Item | Change |
|---|---|
| **Jetson T3000 / T2000** | **NEW** — announced ~Jul 15–16 2026, ship **Q1 2027**, 865/400 FP4 TFLOPS, 32/16GB. Watch List #1. |
| AGX Thor | Gains role as **emulation host** for mid-range Thor modules (JetPack 7.2.1). |
| M5 Ultra | Still not shipping; late-2026 window reinforced (no slip past Oct rumor this week). |
| DGX Spark | FE **$4,699** confirmed sticky; software improved but field UX still mixed → stay Tier 3. |
| Strix Halo / Framework | Still primary SMB; watch full-config price creep vs “$1,999 128GB” headline. |
| Pi AI HAT+ 2 / Orange Pi | Reconfirmed: **not** 7B ≥10 tok/s platforms. |
| RDNA4 / Arc B580 / RTX 5070 Ti | Stable roles; no tier changes. |
| Market | Memory ASP pressure explicitly shaping NVIDIA module ladder + Apple desktop timing. |
| **Recommendations** | **Unchanged primaries:** Orin Nano Super (T1), Framework 64/128GB Strix Halo (T2), Apple M5 Pro/Max efficiency premium. **New 2027 planning item:** T3000 partner systems. |

## 7. Next Run Priorities

1. **T3000/T2000:** Any module MSRP, 1k-unit quotes, or partner mini-PC BOMs; JetPack 7.2.1 emulation perf crumbs.  
2. **Framework 64GB vs 128GB** live US street prices and lead times.  
3. **Mac Studio M5** FCC/invite/leak hardening the Oct window.  
4. DGX Spark stock/ASP and whether partner GB10 undercuts $4,699 FE.  
5. Any independent **Orin Nano Super** vs small Strix Halo power/tok/s bake-off for always-on agent TCO.
